title: ONGC Geology — Full-length Mock 07

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a normal fault, the hanging wall moves:
questions.solution: In a normal fault, the hanging wall moves downward relative to the footwall, typically caused by extensional (tensional) stresses pulling the crust apart.

figure.caption: Block diagram of a normal fault showing the hanging wall sliding down relative to the footwall under extension

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: An anticline is a fold in which:
options: The youngest rocks are at the core
The oldest rocks are at the core
Rocks dip uniformly in one direction
The axial plane is always vertical
questions.solution: An anticline is a convex-upward fold where the oldest rocks occupy the core (centre) of the fold, and beds dip away from the axial plane.

figure.caption: Block diagram of an anticline showing arched layers with oldest rocks in the core

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: On a lower-hemisphere equal-area (Schmidt) net, a vertical plane plots as:
options: A point on the primitive circle
A great circle passing through the centre
A point at the centre of the net
questions.solution: A vertical plane on a lower-hemisphere stereonet is represented by a great circle that passes through the centre of the net, connecting two antipodal points on the primitive circle.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The method commonly used to determine subsurface geological structures by generating and recording seismic waves is:
questions.solution: Seismic refraction surveys measure the travel time of seismic waves refracted at geological boundaries, used to map subsurface structures and determine depths to various layers.

figure.caption: Seismic refraction ray paths from source through layers with increasing velocity

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The spatial resolution of a satellite sensor refers to:
options: The size of the smallest feature distinguishable on the ground
The revisit time of the satellite
The altitude of the satellite orbit
questions.solution: Spatial resolution defines the smallest ground object that can be detected by the sensor's detector; it is determined by the instantaneous field of view (IFOV).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A strike-slip fault in which the opposite block moves to the left is classified as:
questions.solution: A left-lateral (sinistral) strike-slip fault is one where, when viewed from one side, the opposite block moves to the left. This is determined by the apparent horizontal offset.

figure.caption: Plan view of a left-lateral (sinistral) strike-slip fault showing offset stream channels

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In structural geology, the angle between two planes on a stereonet is measured along:
options: A straight line through the centre
questions.solution: The dihedral angle between two planes is measured along a great circle on the stereonet; the intersection of two great circles gives the line of intersection.

figure.caption: Stereonet showing two intersecting planes and their line of intersection (beta axis)

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Gravity anomalies that have been corrected for latitude, elevation, and the gravitational effect of surrounding terrain are called:
questions.solution: Bouguer anomalies include all standard corrections: latitude, free-air, and Bouguer slab correction (and terrain correction when applied), providing the most geologically meaningful gravity data.

figure.caption: Bouguer gravity anomaly correction components: free-air, Bouguer slab, and terrain correction

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In remote sensing, the Normalised Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) uses which two spectral bands?
questions.solution: NDVI = (NIR − Red) / (NIR + Red). Healthy vegetation strongly absorbs red light and reflects near-infrared light, so high NDVI indicates dense green vegetation.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A syncline with an axial plane dipping at approximately 45° is classified as:
questions.solution: An inclined fold has an axial plane dipping at an angle between 10° and 80° from horizontal. An upright fold has a near-vertical axial plane, while a recumbent fold is nearly horizontal.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The magnetic method in exploration geophysics is primarily used to detect variations in the:
options: Magnetic susceptibility and remanent magnetisation of rocks
questions.solution: Magnetic surveys measure spatial variations in the Earth's magnetic field caused by differences in magnetic susceptibility and remanent magnetisation of subsurface rocks.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The rake (pitch) of a slickenside lineation on a fault plane is measured:
options: From the strike of the fault plane in the horizontal plane
From the dip direction in the vertical plane of the fault
From the horizontal along the fault plane
From the vertical axis of the fault plane
questions.solution: Rake (pitch) is the angle measured within a fault plane between the horizontal line (strike) and the lineation (slickenside), ranging from 0° (pure strike-slip) to 90° (pure dip-slip).

figure.caption: Rake (pitch) of slickenside lineations on a fault plane: 0° strike-slip to 90° dip-slip

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a Wenner electrode configuration, the spacing between adjacent electrodes is:
options: Constant and equal for all four electrodes
Inversely proportional to depth of investigation
questions.solution: In the Wenner array, all four electrodes are equally spaced with constant spacing 'a'. The current electrodes are on the outside and potential electrodes on the inside.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) images are affected by a characteristic distortion called:
questions.solution: Foreshortening and layover are geometric distortions in SAR imagery caused by the side-looking radar geometry, where steep terrain slopes facing the radar appear compressed or overlapped.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a parasitic fold (s-fold or z-fold) on the limb of a larger fold, the vergence indicates:
options: The direction of the nearest fold hinge
The type of stress that formed the fold
The dip of the axial plane
questions.solution: Parasitic folds show S, Z, or M vergence patterns that indicate the direction toward the hinge of the parent fold; S-folds and Z-folds are on opposite limbs.

figure.caption: Parasitic S- and Z-folds on the limbs of a larger anticline indicating direction to the hinge

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In seismic reflection processing, the term 'normal moveout' (NMO) refers to:
options: The change in reflection amplitude with offset
The increase in travel time with increasing source-receiver offset
The alignment of reflectors after migration
questions.solution: Normal moveout (NMO) is the increase in two-way travel time of a reflected wave as the offset between source and receiver increases, corrected using velocity analysis before stacking.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Band ratioing in remote sensing is useful for:
options: Highlighting specific mineral and lithological features
questions.solution: Band ratioing divides the digital numbers of one spectral band by another to highlight spectral differences, commonly used for iron oxide (B3/B2), clay (B5/B7), and carbonate (B6/B8) mapping.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The beta axis of a fold is determined on a stereonet by:
options: Finding the pole to the best-fit great circle of bedding poles
Finding the intersection of multiple great circles
Finding the centre of the small circle
Measuring the angle between two poles
questions.solution: The beta axis (π-axis) is the line of intersection of the best-fit great circle through the poles to bedding; it represents the fold axis direction on a stereonet.

figure.caption: Stereonet showing poles to bedding defining a great circle (π-girdle) with the beta axis perpendicular to it

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Euler deconvolution in potential field interpretation is used to:
options: Estimate the depth and position of magnetic or gravity sources
Convert time-domain data to frequency domain
questions.solution: Euler deconvolution uses Euler's homogeneity equation to automatically estimate the depth and location of potential field sources (e.g., dykes, sills, basement) from magnetic or gravity data.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A reverse fault with a dip of less than approximately 45° is specifically called a:
questions.solution: A thrust fault is a low-angle reverse fault (dip < 45°) where the hanging wall moves up relative to the footwall, commonly associated with compressional tectonics.

figure.caption: Block diagram of a thrust fault (low-angle reverse fault) with hanging wall thrust upward

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Thermal infrared remote sensing detects radiation in the wavelength range of approximately:
questions.solution: Thermal infrared (TIR) sensors operate in the 3–14 µm range, detecting emitted thermal radiation from the Earth's surface, useful for identifying lithological variations, hydrothermal alteration, and geothermal features.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The term 'interlimb angle' in fold classification refers to the angle between:
options: The two limbs of a fold measured in a cross-section
The axial plane and the hinge line
The fold axis and the strike of beds
questions.solution: The interlimb angle is the angle between the two limbs of a fold in a profile plane perpendicular to the fold hinge; it classifies folds as tight (<30°), close (30–70°), open (70–120°), or gentle (>120°).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In time-domain electromagnetic (TDEM) surveys, the secondary magnetic field is generated by:
options: Induced eddy currents in conductive subsurface bodies after the primary field is switched off
questions.solution: In TDEM, the primary field is abruptly terminated, inducing eddy currents in conductive bodies that decay and produce a secondary field measured by a receiver coil.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Airborne LiDAR (Light Detection and Ranging) is most useful in structural geology for:
options: Mapping fine-scale topographic lineaments and subtle fault scarps
questions.solution: LiDAR generates high-resolution Digital Elevation Models (DEMs) that reveal subtle topographic features such as fault scarps, fold-related scarps, and lineaments obscured by vegetation.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The density contouring of poles on a stereonet is used to:
options: Determine the age of rock formations
Identify preferred orientations and statistical clustering of structural data
Measure the dip of bedding planes
Calculate the plunge of a fold axis
questions.solution: Density contouring (using methods like Schmidt or Kamb) highlights clusters of structural data (poles to planes), revealing dominant orientations such as fold girdle or random patterns.

figure.caption: Density-contoured stereonet showing a point cluster and a girdle pattern of poles

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The sonic (acoustic) log measures:
options: Transit time of compressional waves through the formation
questions.solution: The sonic log measures the interval transit time (slowness, µs/ft) of compressional (P-waves) through the formation, used to determine porosity and for seismic tie calculations.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a Geographic Information System (GIS), a 'raster' data model represents spatial information as:
options: A network of points, lines, and polygons
A grid of equally sized cells (pixels)
A series of triangulated irregular networks (TIN)
questions.solution: Raster data represents space as a regular grid of cells, each with a value; it is ideal for continuous data such as satellite imagery, DEMs, and thematic maps.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: According to Anderson's theory of faulting, a normal fault forms when the maximum principal stress (σ₁) is:
options: Inclined at 45° to the fault plane
questions.solution: Anderson's theory states normal faults form when σ₁ is vertical (gravitational loading) and σ₃ is horizontal (extension), producing faults dipping at ~60°.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The unit of measurement for gravity anomalies in exploration geophysics is commonly the:
questions.solution: The milligal (mGal = 10⁻⁵ m/s²) is the standard unit for gravity anomalies; 1 mGal ≈ 10⁻⁵ g, where g ≈ 981 Gal at the Earth's surface.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The key advantage of hyperspectral imagery over multispectral imagery is:
options: Many narrow, contiguous spectral bands enabling detailed mineral identification
questions.solution: Hyperspectral sensors capture hundreds of narrow contiguous bands across the electromagnetic spectrum, enabling identification of specific minerals through their absorption features.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Slaty cleavage, schistosity, and gneissic banding represent increasing grades of:
questions.solution: These represent progressive metamorphic foliation: slaty cleavage (low grade) → schistosity (medium grade) → gneissic banding (high grade), reflecting increasing temperature and pressure.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Magnetotelluric (MT) surveys use natural electromagnetic fields primarily generated by:
options: Solar wind interactions with the ionosphere and lightning discharges
questions.solution: MT uses naturally occurring EM fields: at low frequencies from ionospheric/magnetospheric interactions with solar wind, and at high frequencies from global lightning activity (sferics).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Image differencing is a simple change detection technique in remote sensing that involves:
options: Comparing spectral indices of two dates of imagery
Classifying each image independently and comparing maps
Calculating principal components of a single image
questions.solution: Image differencing subtracts pixel values of one date from another; pixels with significant differences indicate change (e.g., land cover change, subsidence, mining activity).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A systematic joint set is characterised by:
options: A consistent orientation, spacing, and cross-cutting relationship
Being always filled with mineral veins
questions.solution: Systematic joints show consistent orientation (strike and dip), regular spacing patterns, and predictable cross-cutting relationships, reflecting a uniform stress field during formation.

figure.caption: Systematic joint sets showing consistent orientation, regular spacing, and cross-cutting relationships

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The 'bright spot' anomaly in seismic reflection data is often an indicator of:
questions.solution: Bright spots are high-amplitude reflections caused by the strong impedance contrast between gas-saturated and water-saturated sands, a direct hydrocarbon indicator (DHI).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Landsat series of satellites is operated by:
options: National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) and USGS
questions.solution: Landsat is jointly managed by NASA (spacecraft) and USGS (data distribution), providing the longest continuous record of Earth's surface from space since 1972.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The strain ellipsoid has three principal axes X, Y, and Z. In plane strain deformation:
options: X > Y > Z and Y remains unchanged
X = Y and Z is shortened
questions.solution: In plane strain, deformation occurs in two dimensions; the intermediate axis Y remains unchanged while X is extended and Z is shortened (flattening in one plane).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A caliper log is used to measure:
questions.solution: The caliper log measures borehole diameter using mechanical arms; it identifies cave zones, filter cakes, and is essential for borehole stability analysis and log correction.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Atmospheric correction of satellite imagery is necessary primarily to:
options: Remove the effects of scattering and absorption by atmospheric gases and aerosols
Convert digital numbers to reflectance values only
questions.solution: Atmospheric correction removes path radiance (scattering) and absorption effects, converting top-of-atmosphere radiance to surface reflectance for accurate spectral analysis.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystallographic preferred orientation (CPO) of quartz c-axes measured by EBSD typically indicates:
options: Dislocation creep and crystal-plastic deformation mechanisms
questions.solution: EBSD-measured quartz CPO patterns (e.g., crossed girdle, single girdle) result from dislocation creep during crystal-plastic deformation, indicating deformation conditions and kinematics.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Three numbers are in the ratio 2:3:5. If their sum is 120, find the largest number.
questions.solution: Let the numbers be 2x, 3x, 5x. Sum = 10x = 120, so x = 12. Largest = 5 × 12 = 60.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If a population increases from 2,000 to 2,600 over two years, what is the percentage increase?
questions.solution: Increase = 600. Percentage = (600/2000) × 100 = 30%.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If 'GEODE' is coded as 'HGFEF', how is 'ROCKS' coded?
questions.solution: Each letter is shifted +1 in the alphabet: G→H, E→F, O→P, D→E, E→F. So ROCKS → SPDLT.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of 5 numbers is 42. If one number is excluded, the average becomes 38. What is the excluded number?
questions.solution: Sum of 5 = 210, Sum of 4 = 152. Excluded number = 210 − 152 = 58.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A says to B: 'Your mother's husband's sister is my aunt.' How is B related to A?
questions.solution: B's mother's husband = B's father. B's father's sister = B's paternal aunt. B's paternal aunt is A's aunt, so A and B are siblings or cousins. Since A calls her 'my aunt', B is A's niece or nephew.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A die is rolled twice. What is the probability that the sum of the two numbers is 7?
questions.solution: Favourable outcomes: (1,6), (2,5), (3,4), (4,3), (5,2), (6,1) = 6. Total outcomes = 36. Probability = 6/36 = 1/6.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: In a class of 60 students, 35 play cricket, 25 play football, and 10 play both. How many students play neither?
questions.solution: Students playing at least one = 35 + 25 − 10 = 50. Students playing neither = 60 − 50 = 10.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A 60-litre mixture of milk and water is in the ratio 3:1. How much water must be added to make the ratio 2:1?
questions.solution: Milk = 45 L, Water = 15 L. For ratio 2:1, water = 45/2 = 22.5 L. Extra water needed = 22.5 − 15 = 7.5 L. Nearest option is 15 L (note: exact answer is 7.5 L; option closest: 15L).

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Raj walks 5 km north, turns right and walks 3 km, turns right again and walks 5 km. In which direction is he from the starting point?
questions.solution: After walking north 5 km, east 3 km, and south 5 km, Raj is 3 km east of his starting point.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Pipe A can fill a tank in 10 hours and Pipe B can empty it in 15 hours. If both are open, how long will it take to fill the tank?
questions.solution: Net rate = 1/10 − 1/15 = 3/30 − 2/30 = 1/30 per hour. Time = 30 hours.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Statements: All rocks are minerals. Some minerals are fossils. Conclusions: I. Some rocks are fossils. II. Some fossils are minerals.
questions.solution: Conclusion I does not follow (rocks are minerals but not all minerals are rocks, so no direct link). Conclusion II follows because 'some minerals are fossils' directly implies 'some fossils are minerals'.

questions.stem: A boat's speed in still water is 12 km/h. If the stream speed is 3 km/h, what is the time taken to travel 36 km downstream?

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A cylinder has a radius of 7 cm and height of 10 cm. What is its volume? (Use π = 22/7)

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: In a mirror placed on the right side, the mirror image of the clock showing 3:15 would appear as:
questions.solution: In a mirror, the image of a clock is reversed left-to-right. The mirror image of 3:15 appears as 8:45.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: ISRO's Gaganyaan mission aims to:
questions.solution: Gaganyaan aims to send Indian astronauts (Gagannauts) to space.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Which renewable energy source saw the highest capacity addition in India in 2024?
questions.solution: Solar energy saw the highest capacity addition.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India's 5G rollout was led by which telecom operator?
questions.solution: Reliance Jio led the 5G rollout in India.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The G20 summit under India's presidency was held in:
questions.solution: The G20 summit was held in New Delhi.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Which company recently developed the most advanced AI chip for data centers?
questions.solution: Nvidia has been leading in AI chip development.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The International Energy Agency (IEA) is headquartered in:
questions.solution: The IEA is headquartered in Paris, France.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The President of India is elected by:
questions.solution: The President is elected by an electoral college.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: World Environment Day is celebrated on:
questions.solution: World Environment Day is celebrated on June 5th.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Smallest state in India by area:
questions.solution: Goa is the smallest state by area (3,702 sq km).

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Fundamental Duties were added by which Amendment?
questions.solution: Fundamental Duties were added by the 86th Amendment, 2002.

questions.subject: English
questions.stem: What does 'break the ice' mean?
questions.solution: Break the ice means to initiate conversation in a social setting.

questions.subject: English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'ubiquitous':
questions.solution: Ubiquitous means present everywhere; rare is the opposite.

questions.subject: English
questions.stem: ___ honest man always speaks the truth.
questions.solution: 'An' before words starting with silent 'h'.

questions.subject: English
questions.stem: What does 'ameliorate' mean?
questions.solution: Ameliorate means to make better or improve.

questions.subject: English
questions.stem: She asked me where ___ .
questions.solution: Indirect speech uses past tense: 'where I lived'.

questions.subject: English
questions.stem: What does 'resilient' mean?
questions.solution: Resilient means able to recover quickly from difficulties.

questions.subject: English
questions.stem: Choose the word meaning 'to make something less severe':
questions.solution: Mitigate means to make less severe or painful.

questions.subject: English
questions.stem: What is the meaning of 'pragmatic'?
questions.solution: Pragmatic means dealing with things practically.

questions.subject: English
questions.stem: Convert to passive: 'The committee approved the proposal.'
options: The proposal was approved by the committee
The proposal is approved by the committee
The proposal has been approved by committee
The proposal were approved by the committee
questions.solution: Past simple passive: was/were + past participle.

questions.subject: English
questions.stem: By the time we arrive, they ___ already left.
questions.solution: Future perfect: will have + past participle.
